cognates.correspondence_path: Ar ك ↔ He כ, Ar ت ↔ He ת, Ar ب ↔ He ב — regular reflexes
Syc ܟ ↔ He כ, Syc ܬ ↔ He ת, Syc ܒ ↔ He ב — regular reflexes

cognates.notes: While often compared to the West Semitic k-t-b root, the Akkadian root appears to be distinct (s-ṭ-r). The semantic link is identical, but the phonology suggests different origins.

caveats: v0 prototype: this is an LLM-generated analysis, not a lexicon lookup. Results may be incomplete or incorrect, especially for Syriac, Tigrinya, and rare forms. Verify against Wiktionary or a scholarly lexicon before citing.
The input 'אכתוב' is the first-person singular future (imperfect) form of the verb 'כתב'. The root is transparently K-T-B.
Akkadian cognate included as a warning of semantic false friends; while commonly glossed as 'write', it is not an etymological cognate of K-T-B.
